type_error_policy: Pycairo's open int-subclass enum construction, runtime subclassing, and Python reflection map to closed MoonBit enums and compile-time rejection. Typed constructors are the primary API; explicitly named raw methods preserve C-int passthrough only where pycairo accepts unknown values. Legacy uppercase enum aliases and Python pickle behavior are language-specific and deliberately absent.

test_type.adaptation: Antialias is a closed MoonBit enum rather than an int subclass. Missing/object arguments, runtime enum subclassing, Python __mro__/__module__ reflection, and construction of unknown raw values are rejected or inapplicable; raw C-int compatibility is exposed only by explicitly named APIs.

test_format_methods.adaptation: MoonBit method syntax is statically dispatched; Python's unbound class-call spelling is not a separate API.

test_surface_observer_mode.adaptation: The portable enum value is exposed even though native observer-surface construction remains an explicit product-scope decision.

test_aliases.adaptation: MoonBit cannot define uppercase enum-typed constants because non-primitive constants are not supported. Typed enum constructors replace pycairo's legacy aliases; primitive module constants remain available where representable.

test_pickle.adaptation: Python pickle compatibility is not part of a native MoonBit value API. Closed enum values have stable typed constructors and Debug output instead.
